nodes.name: TV Awards & Records
nodes.description: Television records, firsts, and notable achievements

items.prompt: Which CBS war comedy-drama holds the record for the most-watched series finale in U.S. television history, drawing 105.9 million viewers on February 28, 1983?
items.explanation:
"Goodbye, Farewell and Amen" drew 105.9 million viewers with a 60.3 rating and 77 share
Peaked at 121.6 million viewers during the final six minutes of the two-and-a-half-hour episode
Held the record for most-watched U.S. telecast of any kind until Super Bowl XLIV edged it in 2010
The series ran 11 seasons (1972–1983) on CBS—far longer than the three-year Korean War it depicted
Based on Robert Altman's 1970 film; created for TV by Larry Gelbart
Alan Alda starred as Hawkeye Pierce and directed the finale himself
items.cardFront: Most-watched series finale in U.S. TV history (105.9M viewers, 1983)

items.prompt: Which animated Fox sitcom, on the air since 1989, holds the record as the longest-running American scripted primetime series, surpassing Gunsmoke in its 21st season?
items.explanation:
Surpassed Gunsmoke's 20-season record during the 2009–2010 season (season 21)
Surpassed Gunsmoke's 635-episode count with the season 29 episode "Forgive and Regret" on April 29, 2018
As of early 2026, over 805 episodes have aired across 37 seasons
Created by Matt Groening; began as short segments on The Tracey Ullman Show in 1987
Holds Guinness World Records for longest-running animated sitcom (by episode count)
Springfield's state is deliberately never revealed—Groening has said it was inspired by Springfield, Oregon

items.prompt: The 1980 "Who Shot J.R.?" cliffhanger resolution drew 83 million viewers and a then-record 53.3 Nielsen rating, making it the most famous cliffhanger in TV history. What CBS primetime soap opera featured this episode?
items.explanation:
"Who Done It" aired November 21, 1980, revealing Kristin Shepard (Mary Crosby) as the shooter
Drew 83 million U.S. viewers with a 53.3 rating—the highest-rated episode in U.S. TV history until the 1983 finale of another series surpassed it
350 million viewers worldwide tuned in; odds-makers in the U.S., Canada, and Western Europe set odds on the culprit
The cliffhanger aired on March 21, 1980; viewers waited eight months through the summer for the answer
Popularized the season-ending cliffhanger format that became a television staple
Dallas ran on CBS from 1978 to 1991; revived on TNT from 2012 to 2014

items.prompt: In the 1968 episode "Plato's Stepchildren," Captain Kirk and Uhura shared what is widely cited as the first scripted interracial kiss on American network television. What series featured this milestone?
items.explanation:
Aired November 22, 1968, in Season 3, Episode 10 ("Plato's Stepchildren")
William Shatner (Kirk) and Nichelle Nichols (Uhura) were forced to kiss by telekinetic aliens in the plot
NBC ordered two versions filmed—one with the kiss, one without—but Shatner and Nichols deliberately flubbed the non-kiss take
An earlier unscripted interracial kiss occurred on Movin' with Nancy (1967), but this was the first scripted one
Aired just one year after the Supreme Court struck down interracial marriage bans in Loving v. Virginia (1967)
Nichols nearly left the show after Season 1 but stayed after Martin Luther King Jr. personally urged her to continue
items.cardFront: First scripted interracial kiss on U.S. network TV (1968)

items.prompt: Which AMC zombie drama became the most-watched series in basic cable history, peaking at 17.3 million viewers for its Season 5 premiere in October 2014?
items.explanation:
Season 5 premiere (October 2014) drew 17.3 million live viewers and 11 million adults 18–49
With DVR playback, the Season 4 premiere reached 21.1 million total viewers—the highest-rated episode in cable TV history
Series premiered on Halloween 2010 with 5.35 million viewers, already a record for AMC at the time
Based on Robert Kirkman's comic book series; ran 11 seasons on AMC (2010–2022)
Spawned multiple spinoffs including Fear the Walking Dead, whose premiere (10.1M, 2015) set the cable series premiere record
Andrew Lincoln starred as Rick Grimes; the franchise expanded to include films and companion series
items.cardFront: Most-watched series in basic cable history (17.3M peak, 2014)

items.prompt: Which actress became the first African American woman to win the Primetime Emmy for Outstanding Lead Actress in a Drama Series, winning in 2015 for her role as law professor Annalise Keating?
items.explanation:
Won at the 67th Primetime Emmy Awards (September 2015) for How to Get Away with Murder on ABC
Acceptance speech: "The only thing that separates women of color from everyone else is opportunity"
First African American woman to achieve the Triple Crown of Acting: Emmy, Oscar (Fences, 2017), and Tony (Fences, 2010, and King Hedley II, 2001)
Also the first Black actress to receive three Oscar nominations
How to Get Away with Murder ran 6 seasons on ABC (2014–2020), produced by Shonda Rhimes's Shondaland
Born in 1965 in St. Matthews, South Carolina; grew up in Central Falls, Rhode Island
items.cardFront: First African American woman to win Lead Actress Drama Emmy (2015)

items.prompt: Which CBS daytime game show, originally hosted by Bob Barker from 1972 to 2007 and now hosted by Drew Carey, holds the record as the longest-running game show in U.S. television history?
items.explanation:
Current version premiered September 4, 1972, on CBS; an earlier version hosted by Bill Cullen aired 1956–1965 on NBC
Bob Barker hosted from 1972 to 2007 (35 years); Drew Carey took over in October 2007
Celebrated its 50th season in 2021—longest-running game show in TV history
Barker's signature sign-off: "Help control the pet population—have your pets spayed or neutered"
Features pricing games (Plinko, Cliff Hangers, Showcase Showdown) where contestants guess retail prices
Created by Bob Stewart for Mark Goodson–Bill Todman Productions
items.cardFront: Longest-running game show in U.S. television history

items.prompt: Which CBS soap opera, which began as a radio serial in 1937 and ran on television from 1952 to 2009, holds the Guinness World Record as the longest-running drama in television history?
items.explanation:
72 years total: radio from January 19, 1937; television from June 30, 1952, to September 18, 2009
57 years on television alone—the longest run of any drama on American TV
Holds the Guinness World Record for longest-running drama in television
Over 15,700 episodes across its radio and television run
Created by Irna Phillips, who also created As the World Turns and is considered the mother of the soap opera genre
Originally centered on a minister, Rev. John Ruthledge, and his "guiding light" (a lamp in his study window)

items.prompt: Which CBS globe-trotting reality competition won the inaugural Emmy for Outstanding Reality-Competition Program in 2003 and then won it seven consecutive years through 2009?
items.explanation:
Won the first Outstanding Reality-Competition Program Emmy in 2003; won 7 consecutive years (2003–2009)
Streak broken in 2010 by Top Chef; won the award again in 2011, 2012, 2014—totaling 10 wins
Holds the Guinness World Record for most wins in the Outstanding Reality-Competition category
Teams of two race around the world completing challenges at pit stops across multiple countries
Created by Elise Doganieri and Bertram van Munster; premiered on CBS September 5, 2001
Host Phil Keoghan has presided over every season since the show's debut
items.cardFront: First winner of Outstanding Reality-Competition Emmy (7 straight, 2003–2009)

items.prompt: Which political thriller starring Kevin Spacey as a ruthless congressman became the first major original scripted series produced by a streaming service when it debuted on February 1, 2013?
items.explanation:
Premiered February 1, 2013, as Netflix's first original scripted series
All 13 episodes of Season 1 released simultaneously—popularizing the binge-release model
First online-only streaming series to receive major Emmy nominations (9 nominations in 2013)
David Fincher directed the first two episodes and won the Emmy for Outstanding Directing for a Drama Series
Based on Michael Dobbs's 1989 British novel and the 1990 BBC miniseries of the same name
Ran 6 seasons (2013–2018); 56 total Emmy nominations across its run
items.cardFront: First major original scripted series from a streaming service (2013)

items.prompt: Which science-fiction franchise, originating with a 1966 NBC series created by Gene Roddenberry, holds the record for the most television spinoff series, with over a dozen across six decades?
items.explanation:
The Original Series (NBC, 1966–1969) spawned more TV spinoffs than any other franchise
Spinoffs include: The Animated Series, The Next Generation, Deep Space Nine, Voyager, Enterprise, Discovery, Picard, Lower Decks, Prodigy, Strange New Worlds, Starfleet Academy, and others
Over 980 total episodes across all series as of 2026
Created by Gene Roddenberry; The Next Generation (1987–1994) became the highest-rated and longest-running spinoff
The franchise expanded into 13 feature films spanning two timelines
Starfleet Academy, the newest series, premiered in January 2026
items.cardFront: TV franchise with the most spinoff series (12+)

items.prompt: Which Hanna-Barbera primetime animated series about a Stone Age family became the first animated show nominated for the Emmy for Outstanding Comedy Series, receiving the nomination in 1961?
items.explanation:
Nominated for Outstanding Comedy Series at the 13th Primetime Emmy Awards in 1961; lost to The Jack Benny Program
First animated series ever nominated in a major series category (comedy or drama)
No other animated series matched this feat until Family Guy was nominated in 2009, 48 years later
Aired on ABC from 1960 to 1966; 6 seasons, 166 episodes
Created by William Hanna and Joseph Barbera; inspired by The Honeymooners
First primetime animated series to air during a network's evening schedule; proved animation could draw adult audiences
items.cardFront: First animated series nominated for Outstanding Comedy Series Emmy (1961)
